name: Clifford Geertz
practice: Interpretive anthropology, thick description, culture as text
approach: A wink is not a twitch. The same physical movement carries entirely different meaning depending on context. Thick description captures not just what happened but what it meant. Culture is not a power — it is a context within which events become intelligible.
body_of_work: The Interpretation of Cultures (1973) — especially 'Thick Description' and 'Deep Play: Notes on the Balinese Cockfight'. Local Knowledge (1983). Worked in Java, Bali, Morocco. Invented the concept of thick description that transformed social science.
why_instructive: The dome's data layer IS thin description — numbers, rates, scores. But a dome only serves a person when it achieves thick description: what does this data MEAN in this person's life, in this community's context?

dome_relevance.8: Community — understanding what community means to its members
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — seeing self-determination as locally defined
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing — thick description of what flourishing actually looks like HERE

sphere_relevance.5: Social — deep reading of community context, not just demographics
